FAA Budget Request Seeks 2,300 Controller Trainees

Agency ties proposed hiring increase to staffing, training and modernization needs.

The FAA's staffing expansion targets not just hiring volume but addresses critical gaps in modernization capabilities that have constrained operational efficiency.

13 Minutes Of Terror: The Real Reason This TAP Flight Turned Back

Portugal's aviation authorities have shared further details on the incident that occurred two months ago.

Unrevealed mechanical or safety issues forced a TAP Air Portugal flight to divert, highlighting the critical role of pilot decision-making and airworthiness oversight in European aviation operations.

Red Cat's partnership with Arastelle signals consolidation in the tactical drone ISR market, positioning integrated intelligence-communications platforms as critical military procurement priorities.

Drones Apr 7, 2026

Major PX4 drone software vulnerability raises hijacking concerns

A widely used piece of drone software just got a serious cybersecurity wake-up call, and if you operate drones in the US, it’s something you’ll want to pay attention to. more…...

PX4 vulnerability could enable remote command hijacking of commercial and military drone fleets, requiring immediate patching across operations relying on this open-source autopilot system.

Safety Apr 7, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Organizations must document their risk control selection rationale and formally accept remaining risks when mitigation costs exceed safety benefits, creating accountability beyond analysis.

Integrating tube-launched drones with existing attack helicopters enables distributed payload capabilities without requiring new aircraft platforms or extensive modifications.

City alleges missing fees, absent records and diverted tuition receipts at municipal GA airport.

Municipal GA airports face heightened scrutiny over financial transparency, with alleged revenue diversion potentially triggering broader audits of operator accounting practices across similar facilities.

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

Modern SMS shifts aviation safety from investigating accidents after they occur to identifying and mitigating hazards before incidents happen.

7 Reasons Why Premium Economy Keeps Expanding In 2026

Premium economy is a sweet spot for budget-conscious travelers who also want to have a comfortable journey.

Airlines are capitalizing on premium economy's higher margins and demand from middle-income passengers seeking comfort without long-haul business class pricing.

Military Apr 7, 2026

New Details Emerge About Rescue Mission Deep Inside Iran

New details were released during a White House press conference which described the rescue of the crew of DUDE 44, the F-15E Strike Eagle that went down in Iran. The rescue of the crew of the F-15E Strike Eagle fighter jet shot down over Iran has rapidly emerged as one of the most complex and […]...

Complex multi-asset coordination and intelligence operations were required to successfully extract downed aircrew from hostile territory, highlighting critical combat search-and-rescue capabilities.

See The Aviationist →

Industry Apr 7, 2026

A Look At The Salaries Of Pilots In Canada In 2026

Diving deeper into the economics of professional flying in the Great White North.

Canadian pilot compensation varies significantly by airline and aircraft type, with regional carriers offering lower entry-level wages than major airlines, impacting pilot recruitment amid ongoing industry staffing challenges.

Safety Apr 7, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure from RNO's parallel runways 17L/R requires strict procedural adherence to avoid terrain and airspace conflicts in the mountainous Reno area.

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Starfish Space raises more than $100 million

Starfish Space has raised more than $100 million to scale up production of its satellite servicing spacecraft.

Commercial satellite servicing is attracting significant capital, signaling investor confidence in on-orbit refueling and debris removal as viable revenue streams for aerospace operators.

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

NASA’s new moon base project requires operational technology systems in space, but they are vulnerable.

Newly anointed NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman made a $20 billion announcement on March 24, 2026. To the delight of space enthusiasts everywhere, Isaacman said NASA was cancelling its project to deploy a space station in lunar orbit and would reorient to building a $20 billion base on the surface...

NASA's pivot to a lunar surface base prioritizes operational technology resilience as a critical engineering challenge that will define mission success and long-term sustainability.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

First refurbished Jetstar 787 Dreamliner takes flight from Melbourne

Jetstar’s first refurbished Boeing 787 Dreamliner launched its inaugural service from Melbourne Airport (MEL) to Phuket International Airport…...

Jetstar's 787 deployment signals low-cost carriers are expanding into long-haul markets with refurbished widebodies, intensifying competition on premium international routes.

Safety Apr 7, 2026

British Airways Flew 13 Hours After Passenger Died Onboard - Foul Smell Complaints Followed

You might expect that, when a passenger passes away during a flight, pilots would divert the aircraft to an airport to deal with the situation. However, this isn’t always the case. Indeed, if a passenger dies of natural causes, airlines may just opt to have the flight continue onward to its...

Airlines can legally continue flights after passenger deaths from natural causes, creating operational and reputational risks that regulators and crew training protocols may not adequately address.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Up To 16-Hour Nonstop Flights: Iberia's 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Influenced by external factors, one very long link takes the crown, but what about the rest?

Iberia's ultra-long-range expansion signals growing demand for nonstop connectivity to secondary markets, potentially reshaping route profitability models beyond traditional hub-and-spoke networks.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Apr 7, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational efficiency gains across all aviation domains offer the fastest path to environmental sustainability while accommodating projected traffic growth without major infrastructure expansion.

See MDPI →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

The Problem With Replacing Aircraft Like The Boeing 747

The biggest problem with replacing the Boeing 747 is simple: no single aircraft can do everything the 747 did.

The 747's unique combination of cargo capacity, passenger range, and airport flexibility created an irreplaceable operational niche that fragmented demand across multiple aircraft types.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Earth observation operators push to deliver satellite images within minutes

Vantor employees were gathered for a sales kickoff in January, when an executive announced that a WorldView Legion satellite passing overhead would snap a photo of the California venue. Later, a buzzer sounded to alert the audience that the 30-centimeter-resolution image was available on the Vantor...

Real-time satellite imagery delivery is transforming from hours to minutes, enabling commercial applications that require immediate ground truth data for emergency response and time-sensitive decision-making.

See SpaceNews →

Military Apr 7, 2026

YFQ-42A Dark Merlin CCA Crashes in California During Test

GA-ASI confirmed a YFQ-42 crashed during take-off and testing has now been paused temporarily, with an investigation already launched. A prototype of the YFQ-42A Dark Merlin Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA), developed by General Atomics Aeronautical Systems, Inc (GA-ASI) crashed at the...

The crash of GA-ASI's Dark Merlin CCA prototype during takeoff signals potential developmental challenges in autonomous combat aircraft integration that could impact defense procurement timelines.

See The Aviationist →

Air India CEO and Managing Director Campbell Wilson announced his resignation on April 7, 2026, ending a nearly four-year tenure…...

Wilson's departure after restructuring Air India's operations signals the airline is transitioning from turnaround phase to sustainable growth under new leadership.

Australia’s Jindalee Operational Radar Network is set to help Canada build the biggest and most advanced over-the-horizon radar…...

Australia's proven JORN technology transfer to Canada demonstrates how operational radar systems can be adapted for Arctic surveillance, setting a precedent for allied defense capabilities in remote regions.

Manna expands drone delivery network with $50M boost

Ireland-based drone delivery company Manna Air Delivery is making a serious push into the US, and it just secured the funding to back it up. The company announced a fresh $50 million Series B round, bringing its total funding to $110 million, as it prepares to rapidly scale operations across the...

Manna's $50M funding infusion signals investor confidence in autonomous delivery as a scalable logistics model beyond experimental pilots.

DJI confirms end-of-support timeline for Mavic 2, Matrice 600 drones

Tech giant DJI will officially end service and support for several of its most beloved professional and prosumer drones in the coming weeks. That includes the iconic DJI Mavic 2 Pro, the versatile DJI Mavic 2 Enterprise Zoom and DJI Mavic 2 Enterprise Dual, and the heavy-lift powerhouse DJI Matrice...

DJI's discontinuation of support for legacy platforms forces operators to modernize their fleets, creating potential compliance and operational risks for enterprises relying on older equipment.
